tables.reasoning:
The global mapping strategy focuses on direct 1:1 table mappings from source to target. All target "Id" columns are populated directly from the corresponding source table's "id". "Legacy_*__c" columns are used to retain the source natural key for verification. Foreign key relationships like "AccountId" and "Account__c" are assumed to be direct ID matches from the respective source foreign key columns (e.g., `contact.accountid` to `account.id`).

Enum values will be mapped case-insensitively after trimming whitespace. If a source value does not match any allowed enum, it will be mapped to NULL. However, if the target column is NOT NULL, a specific default value for that enum will be used (e.g., 'Prospecting' for StageName).

Dates will be parsed from various text formats (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD, DD.MM.YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY) into a consistent 'YYYY-MM-DD' text format. Unparseable or missing dates will be NULL, unless the target column is NOT NULL, in which case '1900-01-01' will be used as a default.

Amounts will be cleaned by stripping currency symbols and handling locale-specific decimal and thousand separators (e.g., comma as decimal point, dot as thousands separator for European format) before casting to DOUBLE PRECISION. Invalid amount strings will result in NULL.

NOT NULL target columns without a direct, guaranteed non-null source will be populated with meaningful defaults (e.g., 'Unknown' for text fields, 'Prospecting' for Opportunity StageName, '1900-01-01' for dates). CreatedDate and LastModifiedDate will be NULL, and IsDeleted will default to 0, as these fields are not present in the source.

All text fields will undergo TRIM. Proper names will use INITCAP (e.g., Name, City, Country, FirstName, LastName, Title), emails will use LOWER, and currency codes will use UPPER for standardization.
This model maps the source account data to the target Account schema, applying specified data transformations. Name is made NOT NULL with a default of "Unknown", and Customer_Tier__c is mapped to its enum values. Legacy_Customer_ID__c is mapped from the corresponding source field, and other fields are trimmed and title-cased as per the plan. CreatedDate and LastModifiedDate are set to NULL, and IsDeleted defaults to 0.

The dbt model for Contact directly maps relevant source columns, applying `TRIM`, `INITCAP`, and `LOWER` as per the transformation guidelines. Enum columns `Role__c` and `Preferred_Language__c` are mapped to their allowed values with case-insensitive comparisons, defaulting to NULL for non-matches. `LastName` handles a NOT NULL constraint by defaulting to 'Unknown' when the source is NULL, and `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` captures the source ID for traceability. Fields not present in the source, such as `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate`, and `IsDeleted`, are handled with NULL or a default value of 0, respectively.
